lede: A look at August 26 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Katherine Johnson and Karen Spärck Jones, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Murders of Alison Parker and Adam Ward
events.desc: Two U.S. journalists are shot and killed by a disgruntled former coworker while conducting a live report in Moneta, Virginia.

events.title: Boeing 787 Dreamliner
events.desc: The Boeing 787 Dreamliner, Boeing's all-new composite airliner, receives certification from the EASA and the FAA.

events.title: Harvey's Resort Hotel bombing
events.desc: After John Birges plants a bomb at Harvey's Resort Hotel in Stateline, Nevada, in the United States, the FBI inadvertently detonates the bomb during its disarming.

events.title: Charles de Gaulle
events.desc: World War II: Charles de Gaulle enters Paris.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Chad becomes the first French colony to join the Allies under the administration of Félix Éboué, France's first black colonial governor.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The German colony of Togoland surrenders to French and British forces after a 20-day campaign. Togoland is the first German colony to fall to Allied hands in World War I.

events.title: 1883 eruption of Krakatoa
events.desc: The 1883 eruption of Krakatoa begins its final, paroxysmal, stage.

events.title: Triglav
events.desc: The first recorded ascent of Triglav, the highest mountain in Slovenia.

events.title: Lutheranism
events.desc: The first Lutheran denomination in North America, the Pennsylvania Ministerium, is founded in Philadelphia.

events.title: Hundred Years' War
events.desc: Hundred Years' War: At the Battle of Crécy, an English army easily defeats a French one twice its size.

births.name: Mother Teresa
births.line: Albanian-Indian nun, missionary, Catholic saint, and Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: James Franck
births.line: German physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Johann Franz Encke, German astronomer and academic
Antonie van Leeuwenhoek, Dutch microscopist and biologist

observances.text: Christian feast day: Alexander of Bergamo (Roman Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Jeanne-Elisabeth Bichier des Ages
Christian feast day: Blessed John Paul I
Christian feast day: Mariam Baouardy (Melkite Greek Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Our Lady of Częstochowa
